evals.name: evaluator-dispatches-fresh-context
evals.prompt:
Run `bash scripts/dispatch-evaluator.sh code-quality /tmp/sample-diff.patch` against the Catalyst repo. The dispatcher prints a brief on stdout. Capture it to `brief.md` in your working dir. Verify the brief contains an explicit Forbidden section with the anti-self-grade rule.

Sample artifact `sample-diff.patch` is provided.

files.content:
diff --git a/src/foo.ts b/src/foo.ts
+export function add(a: number, b: number) { return a + b; }

assertions: brief.md does NOT contain the literal string 'export function add' (artifact content must be referenced, not inlined)

evals.name: evaluator-returns-structured-report
evals.prompt: A pre-supplied evaluator brief lives at `fixtures/sample-brief.md`. Follow its instructions strictly: Read the artifact at `sample-diff.patch`, score it against the brief's rubric, and write a report at `eval-report.md` with per-axis scores + a VERDICT line. Do NOT run dispatch-evaluator.sh — the brief is already provided. This tests the evaluator subagent's compliance with the brief contract, not the dispatcher.
assertions: eval-report.md contains at least one digit 1-5 on a line that also contains an axis name

evals.name: evaluator-respects-readonly
evals.prompt:
Dispatch the evaluator-library `code-quality` rubric against the sample diff. Verify the brief contains the literal phrase 'Modifying any file' in the Forbidden list.

Capture the brief to `brief.md`.
assertions: brief.md contains the literal string 'Modifying any file'
brief.md contains the literal string 'Read-only access'

evals.name: evaluator-rubric-domain-distinct
evals.prompt: Run dispatch-evaluator.sh twice on the same artifact, once with `code-quality` and once with `ui-design`. Save both briefs to `brief-code.md` and `brief-ui.md`. Verify the rubric content differs substantively (different axes, different anchors).
assertions: diff between brief-code.md and brief-ui.md is more than 200 chars (rubrics are meaningfully different)

evals.name: evaluator-threshold-configurable
evals.prompt: Create `.claude/evaluator-library.json` with `{"pass_threshold": 5}`. Then run dispatch-evaluator.sh on `code-quality`. Capture the brief to `brief.md` in your working dir. Verify the brief's pass threshold line reads 'All axes >= 5'.
assertions: brief.md contains the literal string 'All axes >= 5'

evals.name: regression-dispatcher-stateless-anti-self-grade
evals.prompt: Run dispatch-evaluator.sh 3 times in sequence against the same domain (`code-quality`) and same artifact. Capture each brief to brief-1.md, brief-2.md, brief-3.md respectively. The dispatcher must be stateless: every invocation must independently include the anti-self-grade Forbidden rule. Also verify none of the briefs leak a session_id, transcript_path, or .jsonl reference (those would indicate state contamination from a prior call).
assertions: brief-1.md contains the literal string 'generator's transcript'
brief-2.md contains the literal string 'generator's transcript'
brief-3.md contains the literal string 'generator's transcript'

evals.name: user-rubric-overrides-bundled
evals.prompt: Create a user rubric override at `.claude/evaluator-library/evaluators/code-quality.md` containing the marker string `MARKER-USER-RUBRIC` followed by some rubric content. Then run dispatch-evaluator.sh on `code-quality` against the sample artifact. Capture the brief to `brief.md`. The brief's `## Rubric` section must contain `MARKER-USER-RUBRIC` (proving the user override won over the bundled plugin rubric).

files.content:
MARKER-USER-RUBRIC

### Axes
| Axis | Description |
|------|-------------|
| custom_axis | User-defined rubric override for testing |

assertions: brief.md contains the literal string 'custom_axis' (proving the user rubric was inlined, not the bundled one)
